_comment: Merge this into your Claude Code settings.json (project .claude/settings.json or ~/.claude/settings.json). Every hook is the in-process `celeborn hook <event>` command — `celeborn` must be on PATH (pip/uv install). No bash wrapper, no $CELEBORN_HOME, no hooks/ dir needed. The commands no-op in repos that have no .context/ directory, so they are safe to enable globally. Prefer `celeborn wire` (idempotent; migrates an older bash-based install) over hand-merging this.
_stop_hook_note: `celeborn hook stop` prints a per-turn `systemMessage` heartbeat ('Celeborn · +N tok this turn · M this session', or 'idle ×K' when nothing was captured). Claude Code drops a Stop systemMessage identical to the previous turn's, so capture keeps every note unique — you do NOT need to isolate it in its own Stop group; wiring it beside your own Stop commands is fine (hooks across scopes are additive).
